resources.title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
resources.description: Multi-site autonomous web navigation with reproducible infrastructure.

resources.title: BrowserGym
resources.description: Open Gym-style framework for designing, testing, and evaluating browser agents across web task environments.

resources.title: WorkArena: How Capable Are Web Agents at Solving Common Knowledge Work Tasks?
resources.description: Enterprise web-agent benchmark built around common knowledge-worker workflows in browser-based software.

resources.title: VisualWebArena: Evaluating Multimodal Agents on Realistic Visual Web Tasks
resources.description: Multimodal web-agent benchmark focused on visually grounded tasks in realistic web environments.

resources.title: WebShop: Towards Scalable Real-World Web Interaction with Grounded Language Agents
resources.description: Simulated e-commerce shopping from language goals.

resources.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
resources.description: Repository-scale software engineering tasks grounded in real GitHub issues and corresponding pull requests.

resources.title: SWE-Gym: Training Software Engineering Agents and Verifiers
resources.description: Environment for training software engineering agents with real Python repositories, executable runtimes, unit tests, and natural-language issue specifications.

resources.title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
resources.description: GUI-level OS interaction across real applications.

resources.title: τ-bench
resources.description: Customer-service-style domains with APIs, policies, and simulated users for tool-agent-user evaluation.

resources.title: ToolLLM / ToolBench: Facilitating Large Language Models to Master 16000+ Real-world APIs
resources.description: Tool-use framework and instruction-tuning dataset for API-using agents, with training and evaluation components.

resources.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
resources.description: Multi-environment suite spanning OS, databases, knowledge graphs, and digital card games.

resources.title: AgentGym: Evolving Large Language Model-based Agents Across Diverse Environments
resources.description: Framework for training and evaluating LLM agents across diverse interactive environments, trajectory data, and scalable self-evolution.

resources.title: AgentGym-RL: Training LLM Agents for Long-Horizon Decision Making through Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: RL framework for multi-turn interactive agents across real-world scenarios, with emphasis on long-horizon exploration and stability.

resources.title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
resources.description: Multi-step assistant tasks requiring web, tools, and reasoning.

resources.title: AppWorld: A Controllable World of Apps and People for Benchmarking Interactive Coding Agents
resources.description: Stateful API-level “app world” for coding agents.

resources.title: Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard (Gorilla)
resources.description: Leaderboard and tooling for evaluating function-calling reliability.

resources.title: RL environments guide (Hugging Face Space)
resources.description: Interactive walkthrough of what makes an **RL environment for LLMs** (components, design, and scaling in the LLM era).

resources.title: RL for Agents Workshop — Deep Dive on Training Agents with RL and Open Source
resources.description: Hugging Face YouTube session on training agents with RL and open-source stacks (playlist item).

resources.title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
resources.description: Offline control as conditional trajectory modelling; useful when credit is assigned at the sequence level rather than per atomic environment step.

resources.title: ReST^EM: Reinforcement Learning with Self-Training for Language Modeling
resources.description: Iterative grow-and-filter self-training from model rollouts, treating sampled trajectories as data for policy improvement.

resources.title: AgentGym Trajectories
resources.description: Trajectory data for equipping LLM agents with prior interactive capabilities before or during environment-driven improvement.

resources.title: AgentGym-RL / ScalingInter-RL
resources.description: RL framework for long-horizon, multi-turn agent learning with emphasis on stability, exploration, and avoiding behavioural collapse.

resources.title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents
resources.description: Scalable rollout infrastructure for sandboxed trajectory collection and RL training across software engineering, coding, math, and STEM tasks.

resources.title: Agent Lightning: Train ANY AI Agents with Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: Framework for adding reinforcement learning to existing agents by decoupling agent execution from RL training.

resources.title: Mind2Web: Towards a Generalist Agent for the Web
resources.description: Dataset of real-website tasks and action trajectories for training and evaluating generalist web agents.

resources.title: Android in the Wild: A Large-Scale Dataset for Android Device Control
resources.description: Large device-control corpus of human demonstrations across Android apps, for grounding GUI-agent policies.

resources.title: UltraFeedback: Boosting Language Models with Scaled AI Feedback
resources.description: Scaled AI-feedback preference dataset widely used to train reward models and preference-optimised agent backbones.

resources.title: Let’s Verify Step by Step
resources.description: **Process supervision** with outcome and step-level signals—central when rewards come from verifiable intermediate structure, not only final outcomes.

resources.title: PrimeIntellect-ai/verifiers
resources.description: Environments expressed as **Python `assert`s** for training and evaluating LMs with **RL**, emphasising automatically checkable rewards.

resources.title: Agent-RLVR: Training Software Engineering Agents via Guidance and Environment Rewards
resources.description: Extends RLVR to agentic software-engineering tasks, where long-horizon, sparse-reward settings make naive verifier rewards difficult.

resources.title: Awesome RLVR
resources.description: Curated resources on reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards for math, code, and other automatically checkable domains.

resources.title: Tool Learning with Foundation Models
resources.description: Survey connecting tool-augmented reasoning, tool selection, training, and generalisation for foundation-model agents.

resources.title: CodeRL: Mastering Code Generation through Pretrained Models and Deep Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: RL fine-tuning with compiler and execution feedback as the environment signal over program rollouts.

resources.title: R1-Code-Interpreter: LLMs Reason with Code via Supervised and Multi-stage Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: Multi-turn Code Interpreter interaction with curriculum **GRPO** across diverse tasks; bridges tool loops and training infrastructure.

resources.title: RLTR: Reinforcement Learning with Tool-use Rewards
resources.description: RL framework for LLM agent planning that rewards tool-use completeness across multi-step tool invocation sequences.

resources.title: Tool-R1: Sample-Efficient Reinforcement Learning for Agentic Tool Use
resources.description: RL framework for compositional, multi-step tool use through executable code generation.

resources.title: ARTIST: Agentic Reasoning and Tool Integration for LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: Training approach that makes tool invocation and environment interaction first-class operations for agentic LLMs.

resources.title: volcengine/verl
resources.description: **veRL**: performant RL trainer for LLMs (PPO, GRPO, etc.) aimed at large GPU clusters.

resources.title: OpenRLHF/OpenRLHF
resources.description: Distributed RLHF stack with Ray and Hugging Face models.

resources.title: huggingface/trl
resources.description: **TRL**: trainers for SFT, DPO, PPO, reward modelling, and related objectives in the HF ecosystem.

resources.title: OpenPipe/ART
resources.description: Agent reinforcement trainer focused on multi-turn tool use and traced rollouts.

resources.title: OpenReward
resources.description: Open platform for serving RL environments as API endpoints for agent training and evaluation, built around the Open Reward Standard.

resources.title: The Rise and Potential of Large Language Model Based Agents: A Survey
resources.section: Foundations: RL, agents and interaction loops
resources.description: Taxonomy of planning, memory, tool use, and multi-agent behaviour, grounded in agents as systems that act over time under partial observability.

resources.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
resources.section: Foundations: RL, agents and interaction loops
resources.description: **PPO**, a widely used on-policy update for policies that emit discrete tool or dialogue actions in agent trainers.

resources.title: Trust Region Policy Optimization
resources.section: Foundations: RL, agents and interaction loops
resources.description: **TRPO**, constrained policy updates that underpin many stable large-scale agent fine-tuning pipelines.

resources.title: Multi-Step Reasoning with Large Language Models, a Survey
resources.description: Taxonomy of generating, evaluating, and controlling multi-step reasoning, including tool execution and RL-style fine-tuning hooks.

resources.title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
resources.description: Introduces **GRPO** (group relative policy optimisation) for efficient RL on reasoning traces; pairs naturally with agent trainers in [Training systems and infrastructure](#training-systems-and-infrastructure).

resources.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: Large-scale RL (including a cold-start-free variant) that elicits long-horizon reasoning, using verifiable rewards on math and code.

resources.title: Kimi k1.5: Scaling Reinforcement Learning with LLMs
resources.description: RL recipe scaling long-context reasoning with policy optimisation and length control over reasoning rollouts.

resources.title: STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning
resources.description: Self-taught reasoner that filters and reuses model-generated rationales as training data; an early grow-and-filter precursor to RL on reasoning traces.

resources.title: Reinforcement Learning for LLM Post-Training: A Survey
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: Overview of RLHF, RLVR, PPO, GRPO, DPO, and related post-training methods that often initialise or regularise policies later deployed as agents.

resources.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: InstructGPT-style supervised fine-tuning plus **RLHF** with a learned reward model.

resources.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: **RLAIF**-style training using model-generated critiques and preference labels.

resources.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: **DPO**: single-stage preference optimisation without an explicit reward model rollout loop.

resources.title: ORPO: Monolithic Preference Optimization without Reference Model
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: Odds-ratio preference optimisation combined with SFT-style objectives.

resources.title: Model Alignment as Prospect Theoretic Optimization
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: **KTO** alignment from binary favourable-or-not labels.

resources.title: Open Problems and Fundamental Limitations of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: Survey of RLHF limitations, open problems, complementary techniques, and oversight standards for feedback-trained systems.

resources.title: Self-Rewarding Language Models
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: Iterative self-preference and policy improvement using the model as its own preference source—relevant when reward models are co-trained with the agent policy.

resources.title: RLAIF vs. RLHF: Scaling Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with AI Feedback
resources.section: RLHF, RLAIF and preference optimisation for agent backbones
resources.description: Compares human versus AI feedback for preference-model training at scale.

resources.title: Multi-Agent Actor-Critic for Mixed Cooperative-Competitive Environments
resources.description: **MADDPG**, a standard baseline for continuous multi-agent control (conceptual background for mixed agent teams).

resources.title: QMIX: Monotonic Value Function Factorisation for Deep Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: Foundational cooperative MARL method for learning decentralised policies with centralised value factorisation.

resources.title: MAPPO: The Surprising Effectiveness of PPO in Cooperative Multi-Agent Games
resources.description: Strong baseline showing PPO-style methods can be effective in cooperative multi-agent RL.

resources.title: Emergent Tool Use From Multi-Agent Autocurricula
resources.description: Environment-driven curriculum and tool-like behaviour from multi-agent **RL** interaction.

resources.title: PettingZoo
resources.description: Python library providing standardised multi-agent RL environments and APIs.

resources.title: Melting Pot
resources.description: Multi-agent environment suite for studying social interaction, cooperation, competition, and generalisation.

resources.title: OpenSpiel
resources.description: Framework for reinforcement learning and search in games, including self-play and multi-agent decision-making.

resources.title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
resources.description: Role-specialised agents for software tasks (orchestration; often combined with feedback and eval loops).

resources.title: microsoft/autogen
resources.description: Multi-agent orchestration with hooks for human and model feedback (often used adjacent to RL eval loops and population-style training).

resources.title: Video PreTraining (VPT): Learning to Act by Watching Unlabeled Online Videos
resources.description: Behavioural cloning plus **RL** fine-tuning for Minecraft-style agents from large video corpora.

resources.title: MineDojo: Building Open-Ended Embodied Agents with Internet-Scale Knowledge
resources.description: Benchmark and toolkit for open-ended embodied agents in Minecraft-like worlds.

resources.title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
resources.description: Co-fine-tuning VLM objectives with robot actions; bridge between web-scale LMs and closed-loop physical control.

resources.title: Open X-Embodiment / RT-X
resources.description: Large-scale cross-embodiment robot learning effort for generalist robot policies trained across diverse robots and tasks.

resources.title: RoboCat: A Self-Improving Generalist Agent for Robotic Manipulation
resources.description: Self-improving robotic agent that learns across tasks and embodiments through demonstration, data collection, and iterative policy improvement.

resources.title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
resources.description: Visuomotor policy-learning approach using diffusion models for robot action generation.

resources.title: RoboMimic
resources.description: Framework and benchmark suite for robot imitation learning, useful as a bridge between demonstrations and downstream RL fine-tuning.

resources.title: Isaac Lab / Isaac Gym
resources.description: Simulation infrastructure for scalable robot reinforcement learning, imitation learning, and sim-to-real experimentation.

resources.title: RLinf-Co: Reinforcement Learning-Based Sim-Real Co-Training for VLA Models
resources.description: Sim-real co-training approach that warm-starts VLA policies with demonstrations and improves them through RL in simulation.

resources.title: Foundation Models Meet Embodied Agents
resources.description: Workshop resource connecting foundation models, MDP-style robot decision-making, planning, and embodied agents.

resources.title: Who Validates the Validators? Aligning LLM-Assisted Evaluation of LLM Outputs with Human Preferences
resources.description: Mixed-initiative approach for aligning LLM-generated evaluators with human requirements, including criteria drift in evaluation design.

resources.title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models (HELM)
resources.description: Multi-metric, multi-scenario evaluation framework; a reference point for designing broad, comparable agent evaluation suites.

resources.title: AgentHarm: A Benchmark for Measuring Harmfulness of LLM Agents
resources.description: Benchmark of explicitly malicious agent tasks for measuring robustness to misuse in tool-using agents.

resources.title: Reinforcement Learning with a Corrupted Reward Channel
resources.description: Formalises corrupt reward channels (misspecification, sensing errors) and mitigation angles relevant to learned reward models in deployed agents.

resources.title: Goal Misgeneralization in Deep Reinforcement Learning
resources.description: Shows agents can retain capabilities while pursuing the wrong objective off-distribution; directly relevant to reward and environment design for agents.

resources.title: The Alignment Problem from a Deep Learning Perspective
resources.description: Position paper on misaligned goals, deceptive reward-seeking, and power-seeking risks as deep learning systems scale.

resources.title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
resources.description: Early articulation of specification, robustness, and monitoring issues relevant to agent training loops.
